key_findings.kr_effect: KR normalization changed correlation from negative to positive for both simulations
key_findings.v1_improvement: V1 improved from r=-0.09 to r=+0.16 (Δr=+0.25)
key_findings.v2_improvement: V2 improved from r=-0.17 to r=+0.05 (Δr=+0.21)
key_findings.best_model: V1 (hypothetical CTCF) shows better correlation than V2 (literature CTCF) in both raw and normalized space
key_findings.statistical_significance: Neither model achieves statistical significance (p<0.05) even after normalization
key_findings.scientific_conclusion: KR normalization provides fair comparison by removing sequencing depth bias, but model-data discrepancy remains

claude_md_compliance.standard_method: KR balancing is standard Hi-C normalization (Imakaev et al. 2012)
claude_md_compliance.both_normalized: Applied normalization to both experimental and simulation matrices
claude_md_compliance.honest_reporting: Reported results as measured, including non-significant p-values
claude_md_compliance.no_tuning: No parameter adjustment attempted to improve correlation

technical_notes.correlation_sample: Upper triangle only (n=45 pairs), excluding diagonal
